titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/278

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=255 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:39:41.84292+00 | translation moa=254 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:39:47.499965+00

p.cl:
Iocelinus dei gratia Glasguensis Episcopus . Omnibus Sancte matris ecclesie filiis tam presentibus quam futuris . Salutem in
domino.

Nouerit uniuersitas uestra nos ad peticionem  per consensum Rogeri de Scalebroc . dedisse 
concessisse .  Hac carta nostra confirmasse . deo  ecclesie Sancte Marie de Nordberewic .  monialibus
ibidem deo seruientibus In liberam  perpetuam elemosinam . Ecclesiam de kirkebride de Larges . cum dimidia
carucata terre .  cum una salina .  cum duabus acris ad croftum .  cum decimis  oblacionibus  omnibus
aliis ad ipsam ecclesiam iuste pertinentibus . Tenendam ita libere quiete plenarie  honorifice  Sicut
aliqua domus religiosa aliquam ecclesiam in episcopatu nostro Liberius . Quietius . Plenius  honorificentius tenet
 possidet . Concessimus etiam prefatis monialibus ut omnes redditus  prouentus prescripte ecclesie in usus pro
prios . pro uoluntate sua in perpetuum conuertant . Saluis Episcopalibus .

Hiis Testibus . Rad’ . Abbate de melr’ .
Sim’ . Archidiacono . nostro . Herb’ . decano Glasguensi . Cristino decano de Karreic . Helya . Willelmo
Canonicis Glasg’ . Ric’ . clerico de Dundouenald’ . Iohanne . Waltero . Geru’ Clericis nostris . Alex’ . de kuningham . Capellano .
Jocelin, by the grace of God bishop of Glasgow, to all sons of Holy Mother Church, both present and future, Greeting in the Lord:

You should all know that, at the request and by the consent of Roger de Skelbrooke, we have given, granted, and by this our charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at North Berwick, and to the nuns serving God there, in free and perpetual alms, the church of Kirkbride in Largs, with half a ploughgate of land, and with one saltpan, and with two acres at the croft, and with teinds and oblations and all other things rightfully belonging to that church, to be held as freely, quietly, fully and honourably as any religious house holds aand possesses any church in our bishopric. We have also granted to the forementioned nuns that may at will convert all revenues and profits of that church to their own use in perpetuity, saving the episcopal dues.

With these witnesses, Ralph the abbot of Melrose, Simon our archdeacon, Herbert the dean of Glasgow, Christian the dean of Carrick, Elias, William, canons of Glasgow, Richard, clerk of Dundonald, John, Walter, Gervase, our clerks, Alexander of Cunningham, chaplain.
